_meta.description: Per-program rules for placing award flight tickets on hold. Holds let you reserve award space without paying or transferring points yet. Critical for transferable-currency bookings (Amex MR, Chase UR, etc.) where the transfer takes hours to days. Includes 6 programs with reliable holds (AA, Lufthansa M&M, Flying Blue, Cathay Asia Miles, Turkish, Virgin Atlantic), Singapore KrisFlyer as agent-discretionary, and the negative space (most programs do NOT allow holds). Sourced from Frugal Flyer (canonical 2024 list) and Upgraded Points (current 2025 details), cross-checked against airline-specific sources.

schema_notes.max_hold_days: Maximum hold duration in days. null if not allowed.
schema_notes.hold_fee_usd: Fee to place the hold itself. 0 if free. null if not applicable.
schema_notes.phone_booking_fee_usd: Phone booking fee charged when finalizing the held award. Often the real cost since most holds require phone booking.
schema_notes.self_serve_online: true if the member can place the hold themselves online; false means phone agent required
schema_notes.partner_awards: yes | no | varies — whether the program holds partner-airline award seats too
schema_notes.phone_us: US phone number for placing/finalizing holds
schema_notes.recent_changes: Material rule changes in the last 24 months
schema_notes.last_verified: ISO date when this entry was last hand-checked
schema_notes.confidence: VERIFIED | LIKELY | UNVERIFIED per Research Integrity Protocol

_meta.key_concept_use_case: Award holds are most useful when you need to transfer points from a transferable currency (Amex MR, Chase UR, Citi TYP, Cap One) to a loyalty program. Transfers can take from minutes (Amex/Chase) to days (Marriott via Lufthansa). A hold prevents losing the award space while the transfer is pending.

programs.name: American Airlines AAdvantage
programs.restrictions: ONLY program with self-service online hold. Click 'Hold' on the booking-confirmation screen instead of paying with miles. No credit card needed. Reduced from 5-day hold to 24-hour hold in late 2024 to align with revenue ticket policy. The previous 5-day hold was famously generous; the 24-hour version still beats UA/AS/DL who have NO holds.
programs.recent_changes: Reduced from 5 days to 24 hours in late 2024. Existing 5-day holds at the time were grandfathered.
programs.use_case: Hold a Cathay first/business or partner premium-cabin award online without committing miles, then transfer Bilt (1:1, instant) or Citi TYP (1:1, 1-2 days) to AAdvantage to finalize. AA does not partner with Amex MR or Chase UR. Note: Marriott Bonvoy points to AA exists at 3:1 with a 5,000-mile bonus per 60K transferred (4-day delivery). With AA's no-fee change/cancel policy, you can also just book on the spot if miles are available.

programs.name: Air France / KLM Flying Blue
programs.restrictions: Phone booking required for all holds. Up to 3 days (FF) or 'a few days' (UP). $25 USD phone-booking fee charged when finalizing the held ticket. Partner award holds work but are agent-discretionary and not guaranteed.
programs.use_case: Hold an AF/KL or SkyTeam partner award via phone, then transfer Amex MR (instant) or Chase UR (instant) to Flying Blue to ticket. Fee tradeoff: $25 phone-booking fee vs the cost of losing the award space.

programs.name: Cathay Asia Miles
programs.restrictions: Up to 2 days per Frugal Flyer (Upgraded Points reports up to 3 days). $39 USD phone fee when finalizing partner awards. Only Cathay Pacific seats are truly held; partner-award seats may disappear. Award waitlist available alongside holds.
programs.recent_changes: March 2026 devaluation announcement (effective May 2026) changes mileage costs but NOT hold rules.
programs.use_case: Hold a Cathay first/business or oneworld premium-cabin award via phone. Then transfer Amex MR (1-7 days), Citi TYP, or Capital One miles to Asia Miles to ticket. Fee plus uncertainty makes this less attractive than holding via AA for the same award (which has no fee and is online).

programs.name: Lufthansa Miles & More
programs.restrictions: Up to 5 days, free hold, $20 USD phone fee when finalizing. Holds work for Miles & More airlines (LH, SWISS, Austrian, Brussels) AND select Star Alliance carriers. The longest hold of any major program.
programs.use_case: Hold a Lufthansa First or Star Alliance partner premium-cabin award by phone, then transfer Marriott Bonvoy (4 days) or another currency to Miles & More. The 5-day window is enough for slow Marriott transfers.

programs.name: Singapore KrisFlyer
programs.restrictions: Hold duration is at the phone agent's discretion (usually a few days). $25 USD or 2,500 KrisFlyer miles fee. Cannot hold partner awards — only Singapore-operated. Inconsistent experience reported across calls; YMMV.
programs.use_case: Hold a Singapore Suites/First award by phone (Singapore-operated only), transfer Amex MR (1-2 days) or Citi TYP (1-3 days). Worth attempting only for Singapore-operated metal; for Star Alliance partners use Lufthansa or AA.

programs.name: Turkish Miles & Smiles
programs.restrictions: Up to 2 days, free hold (no fee per FF; phone fee varies per UP). Holds partner awards too. Famously bureaucratic — be prepared for multiple call attempts.
programs.use_case: Hold a Turkish-operated or Star Alliance partner award by phone (e.g., the famous TK Hawaii sweet spot), then transfer Bilt (instant) or Capital One to Miles & Smiles.

programs.name: Virgin Atlantic Flying Club
programs.restrictions: Up to 1 day per Frugal Flyer; up to 2 days per Upgraded Points. Free hold, $0 fee on phone booking. Partner awards (ANA, Delta, Korean, etc.) holdable too.
programs.use_case: Hold an ANA First or partner award via Virgin's phone line, transfer Amex MR or Chase UR (1-2 days) to Flying Club to ticket. The most economical hold of the 6 reliable-hold programs since there's no phone-booking fee.

programs.name: United MileagePlus
programs.restrictions: Public award holds were eliminated in 2018. Some elite tiers may get courtesy holds at agent discretion but it is no longer a published policy. United also discontinued the Excursionist Perk Aug 21, 2025. The program has shed several major perks in this period.
programs.recent_changes: Holds eliminated 2018. Excursionist Perk discontinued Aug 21, 2025. Upgrade Award chart removed Nov 24, 2025.

programs.name: Delta SkyMiles
programs.restrictions: No award holds. Delta argues this isn't a problem because Amex MR transfers to SkyMiles in minutes. True for Amex but not for non-Amex transferable currencies.

programs.name: Alaska Atmos Rewards (formerly Mileage Plan)
programs.restrictions: No public hold policy. Atmos Rewards (rebrand from Mileage Plan in 2026) does not list holds in its terms. Phone agents may discretionarily extend a few hours but nothing committed.
programs.recent_changes: Mileage Plan rebranded to Atmos Rewards in 2026 (Alaska + Hawaiian merger).

programs.name: Air Canada Aeroplan
programs.restrictions: No published hold policy. Per Frugal Flyer's 2024 canonical list of programs that DO allow holds, Aeroplan is not included. Earlier reports of '5-day online free hold' appear to have been incorrect or have since changed. If you need to transfer points to Aeroplan, plan accordingly: Amex MR is instant, Chase UR is instant, Capital One miles is instant.

programs.name: British Airways Executive Club
programs.restrictions: No award holds. Phone agents have been reported to occasionally place a temporary hold for a few hours while finalizing partner-award details, but this is not a published policy.

programs.name: Etihad Guest
programs.restrictions: Not on the canonical 2024 Frugal Flyer list. Older reports (milenomics.com 2019) describe agents placing 5-day holds, but Frugal Flyer 2024 and Upgraded Points 2025 do not include Etihad. Behavior may be agent-discretionary on a case-by-case basis but not a published policy.

programs.name: ANA Mileage Club
programs.restrictions: No published hold policy. Workaround: book a Star Alliance partner award via Lufthansa Miles & More (5-day hold) or Virgin Atlantic Flying Club (1-2 day hold) when an ANA-operated flight has the space.

programs.name: Qatar Privilege Club Avios
programs.restrictions: No published hold policy. Workaround: hold Qatar Qsuite via American AAdvantage (24h, free, online) or Cathay Asia Miles (2-3 days, $39).
programs.recent_changes: Qatar moved Privilege Club to Avios currency in 2024.

programs.name: Korean SKYPASS
programs.restrictions: No published hold policy. Workaround: hold Korean Air-operated awards via Virgin Atlantic Flying Club (KE is a VS partner).

decision_rules: When you need to transfer points from a transferable currency to a loyalty program, prefer programs that allow holds. American AAdvantage's online self-serve hold is the easiest. Lufthansa's 5-day hold is the longest.

Amex MR transfers to most partners in minutes (Delta, Hilton, Aeroplan, Flying Blue, Virgin Atlantic, etc.) and 1-2 days for some (BA, ANA). Chase UR transfers are usually instant. Capital One miles transfer in minutes for most partners. So holds matter less for these currencies.
Marriott Bonvoy transfers to airline partners typically take 4 days. ALWAYS use a hold-allowing program when transferring from Bonvoy.
American AAdvantage is the only program with online self-serve holds (no phone needed, no fee, no credit card). Use it whenever the redemption is bookable through AA.

If you cannot hold via the redeeming program, check if the same award can be held by booking through a different partner program. E.g., a Cathay first/business award is bookable via AA AAdvantage, Cathay Asia Miles, OR Alaska Atmos. AA can hold (24h online, free); Cathay can hold (2 days, $39 fee); Alaska cannot hold.
United, Delta, Alaska, and Aeroplan do NOT offer holds. Plan transfers to coincide with booking time.
When in doubt, ask the phone agent. Some programs will discretionarily place holds even when not published policy. Frugal Flyer's 2024 list is canonical for current published policy.

negative_space_summary.definition: Programs that do NOT allow award holds. Either you have the points in your account at booking time or the award is gone.
programs: Etihad Guest (not canonical, but no published policy)
negative_space_summary.implication: Plan your transfer to clear BEFORE booking. Amex/Chase/Capital One transfers are usually instant; Marriott is 4 days; Citi takes 1-3 days. Or use a different program (with holds) to book the same award.

marriott_bonvoy.all_partners: 4 days for transfers to airline partners. 3:1 base ratio with a 5,000-mile bonus per 60K transferred, so 60K Bonvoy = 20K airline miles + 5K bonus = 25K airline miles total.
